name: Maya Chen
title: AI Engineer

signal_clarity.name: Signal Clarity
signal_clarity.description: How effectively they communicate intent to AI — prompt specificity, first-shot accuracy, and context utilization.

evidence: 8% human review/edit rate on AI output
build_stability.name: Build Stability
build_stability.description: Whether AI-assisted code survives production — revert rates, error introduction, test discipline, and code survival.

decision_weight.name: Decision Weight
decision_weight.description: Quality of architectural and technical decisions — planning before building, alternatives considered, and decisions that stick.

recovery_velocity.name: Recovery Velocity
recovery_velocity.description: How quickly they detect and recover from AI errors — systematic debugging, pre-commit catches, and post-recovery stability.

evidence: 139 days of continuous AI usage history
2 AI surfaces with real sessions (usage, not installs)
context_command.name: Context Command
context_command.description: How effectively they maintain context across sessions and tools — reference usage, MCP bridging, session continuity, and retrieval patterns.

orchestration_range.name: Orchestration Range
orchestration_range.description: Multi-tool, multi-model, multi-agent fluency — MCP servers, CLI tools, strategic model selection, and cross-tool workflows.

archetypes.name: Code Weaver
archetypes.description: Clean, surviving AI code — high test coverage, strong edit survival, thorough review.

archetypes.name: System Thinker
archetypes.description: Architecture-first, plan-then-build — high Decision Weight, systematic design with AI assistance.
archetypes.soughtBy: Architecture teams, staff+ roles, technical leadership

archetypes.name: Context Engineer
archetypes.description: Engineers context, rules, memory, retrieval, long-session state. Core driver of Context Command and Signal Clarity.
archetypes.soughtBy: AI product teams, developer experience, platform engineering

archetypes.name: Production Guardian
archetypes.description: Safety rails, production-risk instinct, review-heavy. High Recovery Velocity and Build Stability through test discipline.

archetypes.name: Rapid Prototyper
archetypes.description: Concept to working app — high leverage, features-heavy. Agent leverage is a strength.

archetypes.name: Agent Harness Builder
archetypes.description: Builds and supervises the agent loop — parallel-agent management, output gating, MCP bridges.

archetypes.name: CLI-Native Builder
archetypes.description: Uses AI coding tools beyond the editor — expands Orchestration Range through CLI AI mastery.

archetypes.name: Multi-Agent Orchestrator
archetypes.description: Runs and coordinates multiple agents in parallel — worktrees, subagents, fleet management.

archetypes.name: Integration / MCP Engineer
archetypes.description: Wires MCP servers, connectors, cross-service orchestration. Drives Orchestration Range and Context Command.

businessFit.framing: Fit-to-segment, not a ranking. Scores compare your archetype profile to segment requirements derived from your local sessions + git — never to other builders.

depth.detail: 5 of 6 dimensions on a sufficient sample

enrichment.narrative: Builds production software with AI as a collaborator, not a vending machine: precise, reference-rich direction up front, fast iterative loops, and nothing shipped until it is verified. The signature is strongest in Recovery Velocity and Build Stability, with 91% of AI-authored code surviving in commits.
enrichment.positioningLine: A JavaScript ai_products builder operating mostly at the designs_the_loop level.
whatYouBuilt: Shipped lighthouse-app, an AI-native product (Next.js with the Anthropic SDK wired into the runtime) — the largest body of work, committed steadily across roughly four months.
Built harbor-mcp, a Model Context Protocol server with zod-validated tools that exposes capabilities to coding agents.
Kept tidewater-shop, a React and Express storefront, on a steady release cadence alongside the bigger builds.

decisionPatterns.style: Plans the risky work, then builds in small verifiable steps.

named.name: Plan-before-build on structural changes
named.evidence: Nine written plans precede coding; the router split ran as reversible extraction steps with the suite checked after each move.

named.name: Reproduce, then fix
named.evidence: Failing pricing cases reproduced locally before any change; 96% of flagged errors resolved.

named.name: Test the risky path before merge
named.evidence: An integration test covered the checkout retry and declined-card branch before it shipped.

named.name: Reference-rich direction
named.evidence: Every prompt cites specific files or fixtures; 21.5 turns of deliberate iteration per task on average.

strengths.evidence: 91% of AI-authored lines survive in commits; 96% error-fix rate.
100% reference-rich prompts; 21.5 average turns per task.
Nine specs and plans written before coding; consistent context across Claude Code and Cursor.

growthAreas.observed: Parallelism tops out around four agents at once.
growthAreas.nextSignal: Fan a well-scoped task across more subagents — orchestration grows with breadth, not just depth.

growthAreas.observed: Work concentrates in JavaScript product code.
growthAreas.nextSignal: A second language or a non-product repo would broaden the build-domain footprint.

howYouUseAI.line: Riffs in fast, verified cycles — directs, checks, then ships.

signals.label: Delegation funnel
signals.headline: runs agent fleets
signals.detail: Of 85 sessions: 82% delegate file edits, 82% run terminal commands, 0% reach through MCP tools, 21% dispatch subagents (72 dispatches); peak 4 agent(s) in parallel. The next rung of the funnel is where leverage grows — if the work decomposes.

signals.label: Prompt evolution
signals.headline: steady: 32 → 32 words
signals.detail: Average prompt length per month, 2026-01 to 2026-06. Longer prompts usually mean more constraints stated up front; leaner prompts on the same work can mean the harness now carries the context. Neither is better — the trend is yours.

signals.label: Correction loops
signals.headline: falling: 32 → 17 turns
signals.detail: Average user turns per session by month — you're reaching done in fewer turns. Window 2026-01 to 2026-06.

signals.label: Session rhythm
signals.headline: 20 active weeks, peak 4/wk
signals.detail: Sessions per active week (last ~6 months shown). Bursts and gaps are both visible — this is cadence, not a grade.

signals.label: Model routing
signals.headline: 3 models, 0% multi-model
signals.detail: mostly one model per session: 0 of 85 sessions used more than one model. Routing by task is an orchestration signal; loyalty is a consistency signal.

signals.label: Focus shape
signals.headline: 0% multi-project days
signals.detail: On 0% of active days you touched more than one project — a mostly single-project working style. Interleaving suits orchestration; single-project days suit depth. Different, not better.

signals.label: Context scaffolding
signals.headline: 1 of 3 repos (33%)
signals.detail: Repos with agent context files (CLAUDE.md). Scaffolded repos let agents start warm; the unscaffolded ones are the cheapest leverage still on the table.

signals.label: Harness inventory
signals.headline: 1 skills
signals.detail: Agent tooling across your repos: 1 skills. CLAUDE.md in 1 repos (3 lines total). This is the craft of making agents start warm — skills, subagents, commands, and hooks are reusable leverage.

signals.label: Solo-equivalent time
signals.headline: ~336–505h
signals.detail: Your 253 measured hands-on hours shipped 13,620 AI-authored lines (91.9% of tracked output). Solo-equivalent time is a research-anchored BAND (studies report ~25–50% task-time savings with AI assistance; evidence is mixed — one 2025 RCT found experienced devs slower on familiar code), applied to your measured hands-on hours. An estimate, never a score.

sources.label: Other AI tools (VS Code Copilot/Cline/Cody, Continue, Aider, Windsurf, Zed, JetBrains)

codeScan.gap: Local code scan (structure, deps, tests, deploy configs) not run.

gaps.gap: Local code scan (structure, deps, tests, deploy configs) not run.

summaryLine: Iterates in Prompt Iterate mode — strongest in Recovery Velocity and Build Stability, 4 parallel agents.

leverage.basis: Per-commit authorship attribution over 60 tracked commits (Cursor scored commits — commits without attribution are not counted).
leverage.estimateNote: Solo-equivalent time is a research-anchored BAND (studies report ~25–50% task-time savings with AI assistance; evidence is mixed — one 2025 RCT found experienced devs slower on familiar code), applied to your measured hands-on hours. An estimate, never a score.
